entries.title: Printing Press
entries.content: Johannes Gutenberg (~1440) enabled mass production of books in Europe, spreading literacy and Reformation ideas.

entries.title: Electric Light Bulb
entries.content: Thomas Edison commercialized practical incandescent bulb (1879). Revolutionized work hours, safety, and industry.

entries.title: Internet Origins
entries.content: ARPANET (1969) evolved into global Internet. TCP/IP (1980s), World Wide Web (Tim Berners-Lee, 1989).

entries.title: Penicillin
entries.content: Alexander Fleming discovered antibiotic properties (1928). Transformed medicine by treating bacterial infections.

entries.title: Wheel
entries.content: One of earliest technologies (~3500 BCE Mesopotamia). Enabled transport, pottery wheels, and machinery.
